dialogos.texto_en: I feel like I'm not in Japan, no matter where I look.\nI wonder if the real America is like this too?
I don't know. America is vast,\nand the climate varies depending on the location.
That's true. Hawaii and Alaska are both in America too.
Come to think of it, where is Hollywood located?
Hollywood is in Los Angeles.\nIt's on the West Coast, on the Pacific Ocean.
But why the sudden change of topic?
No, I mean, when you think of America,\nit's the home of cinema, right?
Well, yeah, Hollywood is there,\nand it has that kind of image.
Recently, I've been thinking.\nIf Ichika continues to be an actress%p-2;――
She might eventually become a world-famous actress\nand go abroad, right?
Well, that's a premature discussion.
However, it's not impossible, is it?
Well, yeah. If she keeps challenging herself,\neventually, that kind of thing might happen.
Yes. Ichika takes her work as an actress seriously.
But if that happens,\nshe won't be able to live with us\nlike she does now, will she?
Of course, it's not just Ichika.\nThe others might also graduate from university\nor vocational school, and move out.
Someday, the days when we all lived together\nwill come to an end.
Itsuki...
Everyone will live in different places.\nThat future will end up coming, someday...
And you'll feel lonely?
Of course.\nWe've been together all this time, after all.
Oh, I see. That's true.
(I guess... It's not like I'll be lonely\n going to Tokyo and leaving home.)
Whatever the future holds, when the day comes\nfor Ichika to set off in pursuit of her dreams,\nlet's celebrate in a grand fashion.
Again, isn't that premature?
Someday, that day will come.\nYou may be quintuplets, but each of you\nare an individual person as well.
That's true.
When we were little, we looked exactly alike,\nbut eventually we developed our own preferences\nand changed our hairstyles...
Of course, that doesn't mean I don't like that.
We can learn things from each other\nbecause we're all different.\nAnd there are four of us to learn from.
But there was nothing you could do\nabout your inability to study,\neven when all five of you pooled your efforts.
Ooohh...\nI have no response to that.
Whatever the future holds, when the day comes\nfor Ichika to set off in pursuit of her dreams,\nlet's celebrate in a grand fashion.
Again, isn't that premature?
Yet, there are things that'll never change.
Things that will never change...\nAre you talking about us being sisters?
Yeah, that's what I mean.\nIf something happens to one of you,\nthe other four would come running to help, right?
Yes, I would be the first.
You'd come running too, Uesugi-kun, right?
...Are you okay with that?
Are you saying that to be mean?
Of course, it's fine.\nYou also cherish my dear sisters.\nThat makes me very happy.
Whatever the future holds, when the day comes\nfor Ichika to set off in pursuit of her dreams,\nlet's celebrate in a grand fashion.
Again, isn't that premature?
Thinking about it wouldn't hurt, right?\nAfter all, they say it's better to be prepared.
Above all, I don't want to see Ichika off\nwith a sad face as she chases her dream.
But, isn't there another concern\nthat should be addressed first?
Another one?
Well, if she's going to America,\nthere's no way Ichika's English skills\nwill be good enough to get by.
Y-yes, indeed.\nThat's a huge problem...
Well then, why don't you teach her?
Oh? Are you saying that you trust me enough\nto become her teacher, Uesugi-kun?
Ooh...
(Actually, the way I said that just now,\n it sounds like there isn't any doubt,\n that I trust her completely.)
What do you think, Uesugi-kun?\nPlease tell me your honest opinion.
(Kkh... With that little grin...)
W-well, I think you'll be able\nto make your dreams come true,\nso I bet you can become her teacher.
Teehee!\nThank you very much.\nI'll make sure to live up to your expectations.
